top_signals.title: Three reasons why a food-supply shock may be coming to Southeast Asia: Goldman Sachs
top_signals.summary: Higher input costs and a potential El Niño weather event are combining to increase the risk of significant food price inflation across Southeast Asia, according to a report from Goldman Sachs
top_signals.why_it_matters: Inflation expectations impacting rate path, bonds and global risk assets.
